title: SACRAMENTO – Assemblymember Dr. Joaquin Arambula (D-Fresno) today announced that he has chosen Fresno Building Healthy Communities as the 2024 Nonprofit of the Year for the 31st As

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Fresno Building Healthy Communities works to lift up historically marginalized communities by engaging residents in proactive efforts on health care, education, land use, safe transportation, and development.",
    "Fresno BHC was established in 2010 under The California Endowment, which focuses on equity by expanding access to affordable quality health care in underserved and disadvantaged communities.",
    "Fresno BHC has a network of more than 30 nonprofit and faith-based organizations that reaches over 97,000 people in central, southeast, and southwest Fresno.",
    "Fresno BHC co-authored and led community and voter engagement efforts for Measure P, a tax measure in the City of Fresno for parks, trails, and the arts.",
    "Fresno BHC secured a $6.6 million grant from the California Department of Parks and Recreation to renovate Calwa Park in southeast Fresno.",
    "Fresno BHC co-developed the Fresno COVID-19 Equity Project, which provided vaccines, preventive education, and resources to more than 25,000 residents in Fresno County during the pandemic.",
    "Assemblymember Arambula has been at the forefront of efforts to expand health care to all people in California, regardless of immigration status."
  ]
}
```
